[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [38°08′09″S 176°15′11″E﻿ / ﻿38.1358348°S 176.2529753°E﻿ / -38.1358348; 176.2529753](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Rotorua_Lakes_Council&params=38.1358348_S_176.2529753_E_)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Territorial authority of New Zealand

| Rotorua Lakes Council   *Te Kaunihera o ngā Roto o Rotorua* | |
| --- | --- |
| [Coat of arms or logo](/wiki/File:Rotorua_coat_of_arms_full_achievement.svg) | |
| [Logo](/wiki/File:Rotorua_Lakes_Council_logo.png) | |
| Type | |
| Type | [Unicameral](/wiki/Unicameral "Unicameral") of [Rotorua District](/wiki/Rotorua_District "Rotorua District") |
| Houses | Governing Body |
| Term limits | None |
| History | |
| Founded | 6 March 1989 (1989-03-06) |
| Structure | |
| Seats | 11 seats (1 mayor, 10 ward seats) |
| Length of term | 3 years |
| Website | |
| [rotorualakescouncil.nz](http://rotorualakescouncil.nz) | |

**Rotorua Lakes Council** or **Rotorua District Council** ([Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_language "Māori language"): *Te Kaunihera o ngā Roto o Rotorua*) is the [territorial authority](/wiki/Territorial_authorities_of_New_Zealand "Territorial authorities of New Zealand") for the [Rotorua District](/wiki/Rotorua_District "Rotorua District") of [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand").[[1]](#cite_note-aboutcouncil-1)

The council is led by the [mayor of Rotorua](/wiki/Mayor_of_Rotorua "Mayor of Rotorua"), who is currently [Tania Tapsell](/wiki/Tania_Tapsell "Tania Tapsell")[[2]](#cite_note-2). There are also 10 ward councillors.[[1]](#cite_note-aboutcouncil-1)

## Composition

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_Council&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Composition")]

### Councillors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_Council&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Councillors")]

Rotorua Lakes Council, 2022–2025[[3]](#cite_note-councillors-3)[[4]](#cite_note-2022_results-4)

| Position | Name | Ward | [Ticket](/wiki/Ticket_(election) "Ticket (election)") (if any) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Mayor](/wiki/Mayor_of_Rotorua "Mayor of Rotorua") | [Tania Tapsell](/wiki/Tania_Tapsell "Tania Tapsell") | [At-large](/wiki/At-large "At-large") |  |
| Deputy Mayor | Sandra Kai Fong | General |  |
| Councillor | Gregg Brown | General |  |
| Councillor | Robert Lee | General | Rotorua District Residents & Ratepayers |
| Councillor | Conan O'Brien | General | [Independent](/wiki/Independent_politician "Independent politician") |
| Councillor | Don Paterson | General | Saving Rotorua |
| Councillor | Fisher Wang | General | Independent |
| Councillor | Lani Kereopa | [Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_wards_and_constituencies "Māori wards and constituencies") |  |
| Councillor | Trevor Maxwell | Māori |  |
| Councillor | Rawiri Waru | Māori |  |
| Councillor | Karen Barker | Rural | Community Focused Leadership |

### Community boards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_Council&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Community boards")]

The Rotorua Lakes Council has created two [community boards](/wiki/Community_boards_in_New_Zealand "Community boards in New Zealand"), under the provisions of Part 4 of the [Local Government Act 2002](/wiki/Local_Government_Act_2002 "Local Government Act 2002"),[[5]](#cite_note-5) to represent specific communities in Rotorua:

* Rotorua Lakes Community Board (established in 2007)
* Rotorua Rural Community Board (established in 2016)

Each community board consists of four elected members and a councillor appointed by the council, and is intended to provide advice to the city council regarding the interests of their communities.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Coat of arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_Council&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Coat of arms")]

A [coat of arms](/wiki/Achievement_(heraldry) "Achievement (heraldry)") was granted by the [College of Arms](/wiki/College_of_Arms "College of Arms") to the city of Rotorua on 10 December 1963. At the time it was granted, it was believed to be the only city in New Zealand with a motto in [Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_language "Māori language"). The motto is credited to Pakeke Leonard, a former deputy mayor of Rotorua.[[8]](#cite_note-arms-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)

On 2 April 1979, the arms were subsequently adopted at the first meeting of the Rotorua District Council.[[8]](#cite_note-arms-8) The [blazon](/wiki/Blazon "Blazon") for the arms is:

Coat of arms of Rotorua

|  |
| --- |
| Crest  On a [wreath](/wiki/Torse "Torse") of the colours a male [huia bird](/wiki/Huia "Huia") standing in a brake of [New Zealand fern](/wiki/Silver_fern "Silver fern") [proper](/wiki/Tincture_(heraldry)#Proper "Tincture (heraldry)").  Escutcheon  [Azure](/wiki/Azure_(heraldry) "Azure (heraldry)") on a [chevron](/wiki/Chevron_(insignia) "Chevron (insignia)") [Or](/wiki/Or_(heraldry) "Or (heraldry)") a [rainbow trout](/wiki/Rainbow_trout "Rainbow trout") leaping proper between in [chief](/wiki/Chief_(heraldry) "Chief (heraldry)") a [pine tree](/wiki/Pine "Pine") and a sprig of [kōwhai](/wiki/K%C5%8Dwhai "Kōwhai") [leaved slipped and flowered](/wiki/Charge_(heraldry)#Plants "Charge (heraldry)") and a [geyser](/wiki/Geyser "Geyser") issuing from rock in [base](/wiki/Escutcheon_(heraldry)#Points "Escutcheon (heraldry)") all also proper.  Supporters  On the [dexter](/wiki/Dexter_and_sinister "Dexter and sinister") side a figure representing a farm settler of the nineteenth century, at his feet a [cattle dog](/wiki/Australian_Cattle_Dog "Australian Cattle Dog") [sejant](/wiki/Attitude_(heraldry)#Sejant "Attitude (heraldry)") and on the [sinister](/wiki/Dexter_and_sinister "Dexter and sinister") side a figure representing a [Māori chieftain](/wiki/Rangatira "Rangatira") all proper.  Motto  *Tātau tātau* ("We together") |
